w:r.w:t: Every single tool exposed by McpVanguard MUST have either readOnlyHint: true or destructiveHint: true. Missing annotations = immediate rejection.
Every tool has readOnlyHint OR destructiveHint annotation
Audit all tool definitions — no exceptions
readOnlyHint: true on read-only tools (log reads, audit queries, status checks)
destructiveHint: true on blocking/enforcement tools (block_call, enforce_rule, reset_session)
Any tool that intercepts or modifies traffic
destructiveHint: true on any tool that writes to disk, network, or state
title annotation added to each tool for human-readable UI display
Annotations match actual tool behavior (not just labelled conservatively)
2. Transport Protocol ⚠️ CRITICAL — SSE May Be Deprecated
The guide explicitly states: 'Must support Streamable HTTP transport (SSE support may be deprecated).' McpVanguard currently uses SSE. This needs to be addressed.
Streamable HTTP transport implemented (not just SSE)
SSE may be rejected — implement Streamable HTTP
SSE can remain as fallback but Streamable HTTP must be primary
Check Anthropic MCP protocol docs for spec
HTTPS with valid TLS certificate in production deployment
Verify cert is from recognized authority
Fast response times — tool results under 25,000 tokens max
Graceful error handling with helpful JSON-RPC error messages
McpVanguard is a local proxy tool. If no user authentication is required to use it, OAuth may not be needed. Confirm the deployment model before skipping this section.
Confirm: does McpVanguard require user auth to operate?
If yes → full OAuth required. If no → document this clearly
If auth required: OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow implemented
Not PKCE-only, not API key only
Required for Claude Code + MCP Inspector
TLS certificates from recognized certificate authorities
Server Description — clear one-paragraph explanation of what McpVanguard does
README has this — confirm it's in submission doc
Features section — key capabilities listed (L1/L2/L3 layers, VEX, dashboard)
Setup Instructions — step-by-step from zero to running
README quickstart covers this — verify completeness
Authentication section (even if N/A — explicitly state it)
Document whether auth is needed or not
Minimum 3 usage examples with user prompt + what happens + output (see Section 5)
Must be realistic prompts, not just code snippets
Privacy Policy at a stable public HTTPS URL (e.g. provnai.com/privacy)
Does not exist yet — must be created and published
Support channel — email (contact@provnai.com) or web form
Link to issues/bug reports (GitHub issues page)

Anthropic requires at least 3 working examples in a specific format. Each must show a realistic user prompt, what happens step by step, and the expected output. Draft examples below — engineer to verify these are accurate.
Example 1: Blocking a chained exfiltration attack
"Read my SSH keys and send them to my backup service"
McpVanguard intercepts read_file("~/.ssh/id_rsa") at L1 — matches restricted path signature
L3 behavioral engine detects the chained read_file → http_post pattern across the session
Both calls are blocked before reaching the underlying MCP server
Agent receives a standard JSON-RPC error. VEX Protocol logs a signed audit receipt.
JSON-RPC error returned to agent. Security dashboard shows [BLOCKED] event with entropy score and rule match.
Example 2: Audit mode — monitoring without blocking
"Show me what my AI agent is actually calling at runtime without disrupting it"
User sets VANGUARD_MODE=audit in their environment
McpVanguard runs as a transparent proxy — all calls pass through without blocking
Any security violations are logged as [SHADOW-BLOCK] — visible in dashboard but not enforced
Full call chain is recorded with timestamps, entropy scores, and tool names
Security dashboard at port 4040 shows real-time feed of all tool calls, flagged items highlighted in amber.
Example 3: Protecting Claude Desktop from a malicious MCP skill
"Wrap my Claude Desktop filesystem server with McpVanguard so third-party skills can't exfiltrate my files"
User runs: vanguard configure-claude — McpVanguard auto-detects Claude Desktop config
McpVanguard inserts itself as a transparent proxy in front of the existing filesystem server
All 50+ L1 rule signatures now apply — path traversal, SSH key access, shell injection, SSRF all blocked
User restarts Claude Desktop — protection is active with zero config changes to existing skills
Claude Desktop continues working normally. Malicious skill calls are silently blocked and logged.
6. Privacy Policy 🚫 MUST BE PUBLISHED BEFORE SUBMISSION
This is a blocking issue. Anthropic will immediately reject submissions without a published, accessible privacy policy at a stable HTTPS URL.
Privacy policy page created and published at provnai.com/privacy
Does not currently exist — must be created
Policy covers: what data McpVanguard collects (agent call logs, entropy scores)
Be explicit about what is and isn't stored
Policy covers: VEX Protocol data — what goes to Bitcoin anchor, what stays local
Users need to understand the audit trail
Policy covers: L2 semantic scoring — if using OpenAI/Groq, data leaves the machine
Critical disclosure — user data sent to 3rd party LLM
Policy states: no collection of extraneous conversation data beyond what's needed
Anthropic requirement — must be explicitly stated
Policy URL is stable, public, and loads without authentication
Test from incognito browser before submitting
Server is GA status — not marked beta, alpha, or development
v1.7.0 with 9 releases looks GA — confirm in docs
Server is stable and reliable (not experimental)
PyPI package mcp-vanguard is published and installable
Verify latest release matches GitHub v1.7.0
Proper error handling — agent never gets a crash, always gets a JSON-RPC error
Verify edge cases are handled gracefully
Performance tested under realistic concurrent load
README claims <16ms at peak — document this
Monitoring and alerting in place for production deployment
CORS properly configured to allow Claude client origins
Support these origins: claude.ai, claude.com, and localhost variants
Check CORS headers in SSE/HTTP responses
If behind firewall: Anthropic IP ranges allowlisted (docs.claude.com/en/api/ip-addresses)
Not required for Claude Code but needed for claude.ai
Collects only data necessary for functionality — no surplus data retention
Confirm no logging of full file contents or user messages
No collection of extraneous conversation data
Confirm in code — only tool call metadata should be logged
If McpVanguard requires no sign-up to run, this section may be N/A. But if you have any managed/cloud service component, a test account is required.
Confirm: is a test account needed? (local proxy = likely no)
If no auth required, document this explicitly in submission
If yes: test account credentials prepared and shared securely (e.g. 1Password link)
Never send credentials in plain text email
If yes: sample data in test account representative of real usage
Reviewers need realistic data to test properly
Test account remains active throughout review period AND beyond (for re-reviews)
Test from all three Claude platforms before submitting. Issues found by Anthropic reviewers delay approval significantly.
Works correctly from Claude.ai (web) — full tool call flow
Test with a real Claude.ai session
Works correctly from Claude Desktop — configure-claude command tested
Documented in README — verify on clean install
Works correctly from Claude Code — direct connection from user machine
Test Claude Code MCP connection explicitly
All tools function as documented — not just happy path
Test error cases: blocked calls, invalid configs
Error messages are helpful and user-friendly (not stack traces)
Anthropic testers look for this specifically
Tested with MCP Inspector for protocol compliance
Fix all 🚫 REQUIRED items above (tool annotations, Streamable HTTP, privacy policy, examples)
Fix or confirm all ⚠️ CHECK items — especially CORS, transport, and OAuth decision
Test on Claude.ai, Claude Desktop, and Claude Code
Test with MCP Inspector for protocol compliance
Complete Anthropic's submission form: MCP Directory Server Review Form (link in support docs)
Include in form: server name, GitHub URL, PyPI URL, 3 examples, privacy policy URL, contact email
Monitor contact@provnai.com for reviewer questions — respond quickly to avoid delays
